title: DTAA explained: How NRIs can avoid double taxation and save on taxes? - Mint
summary: DTAA explained: How NRIs can avoid double taxation and save on taxes? Mint
ai_summary: This item explains how Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) can leverage Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements (DTAAs) to mitigate double taxation and optimize their tax liabilities. It provides a practical guide on the mechanisms within DTAAs that prevent income from being taxed in both India and their country of residence.

title: The global minimum tax: More winners than losers – even without the US - CEPR
summary: The global minimum tax: More winners than losers – even without the US CEPR
ai_summary: This analysis from CEPR examines the economic impact of the global minimum tax (Pillar Two GloBE rules), concluding that more countries are likely to be revenue winners than losers, even if the United States does not fully implement its domestic minimum tax. It models potential revenue shifts and competitive implications under various scenarios, offering a nuanced perspective on the global tax landscape.

title: What does Kuwait’s OECD QDMTT recognition mean for multinationals? - Kuwait Times
summary: What does Kuwait’s OECD QDMTT recognition mean for multinationals? Kuwait Times
ai_summary: Kuwait has received OECD recognition for its Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (QDMTT), signaling a significant step towards its Pillar Two implementation. This recognition impacts multinational enterprises operating in Kuwait, requiring them to factor Kuwait's QDMTT into their global minimum tax calculations and potentially reducing the top-up tax payable in other jurisdictions under the Income Inclusion Rule (IIR) or Undertaxed Profits Rule (UTPR).
